message:
fix(ffs-mvcc): increase adaptive policy warmup to 50 commits and fix vendor fuser links

- Increase AdaptivePolicyConfig warmup_commits from 20 to 50: the previous
  threshold of 20 commits yields <2.3 effective Bayesian samples, which is
  statistically insufficient for reliable policy switching decisions. The
  new default of 50 provides more credible evidence before engaging adaptive
  behavior (bd-83ml)
- Update vendor/fuser README.md and fuse_abi.rs doc links from master to
  main branch per project convention (bd-zgjb)
- Update beads issue tracker with new closed tasks

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-7tn4","title":"PoR for cryptographic durability audit (Graveyard Entry 11.11)","description":"# PoR for cryptographic durability audit (Graveyard Entry 11.11)

## GOAL
Implement Proofs of Retrievability (PoR) for cryptographic verification that stored data is intact and retrievable, without reading the entire dataset.

## BACKGROUND
From Alien CS Graveyard 11.11: PoR (Juels-Kaliski 2007, Shacham-Waters 2008) allows a verifier to challenge a storage system with random queries. The storage system responds with proofs that specific blocks exist and are correct. If the proofs verify, the entire dataset is retrievable with high probability.

## DESIGN
- Challenge: verifier sends random block indices + random coefficients
- Response: storage computes linear combination of challenged blocks weighted by coefficients
- Verification: verifier checks response against precomputed authenticators
- Authenticators: computed once at file creation, stored alongside data

## APPLICABILITY
- Future feature: useful for cloud/remote storage verification
- Complementary to RaptorQ: PoR proves data exists, RaptorQ repairs it if it doesn't
- Useful for auditing: periodic PoR challenges prove filesystem integrity without full scan

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- [ ] PoR authenticator generation at file creation
- [ ] Challenge-response protocol implemented
- [ ] Verification: false positive rate < 2^-128
- [ ] Benchmark: challenge/response time for 1GB file","acceptance_criteria":"PoR authenticator generation at file creation time. Challenge-response protocol implemented with configurable challenge count. Verification: false positive rate below 2^-128. Authenticators stored alongside data in reserved metadata area. Benchmark: challenge/response time for 1GB file. Benchmark: authenticator generation time per MB. Unit tests: generate authenticators, challenge, verify for small files. Unit tests: corrupted block causes verification failure. Unit tests: uncorrupted file passes all challenges. Property test: random challenge sets always verify on intact data.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralThrush","created_at":"2026-02-13T09:30:32.840799452Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-18T06:37:09.585683263Z","closed_at":"2026-02-18T06:37:09.585575381Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crypto","durability","por"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7tn4","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:05:32.828805088Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":203,"issue_id":"bd-7tn4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

Cluster: repair_durability
Mapped graveyard sections: 1.1 RaptorQ + 12.13 Change-point + 11.11 PoR
EV score: 25 (Impact*Confidence*Reuse / (Effort*Friction))
Priority tier: S/A
Budgeted mode: decode_budget_ms=200, io_budget_mb=64, max_repair_attempts=3
Fallback trigger: read-only degrade + evidence ledger + manual repair workflow

- Isomorphism proof block per lever.
- Evidence-ledger event for decision path + fallback invocation.
- Repro pack: env.json, manifest.json, repro.lock (and LEGAL.md if IP risk appears).","created_at":"2026-02-13T23:09:32Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-820","title":"Implement RaptorQ symbol refresh on dirty group flush","description":"# Implement RaptorQ symbol refresh on dirty group flush

## GOAL
Ensure repair symbols are regenerated when block groups are modified, as mandated by spec §0.4: "A block write that never triggers repair symbol refresh for its group is a spec-conformance bug."

## BACKGROUND
Currently repair symbols are generated at image creation. When blocks are modified:
1. Group becomes "stale" (symbols no longer match data)
2. Must regenerate before corruption can be recovered
3. Two modes: lazy (on next scrub) or eager (on fsync)

## TECHNICAL REQUIREMENTS

### 1. Dirty Group Tracking
```rust
pub struct DirtyGroupTracker {
    dirty: BitVec,  // One bit per group
}

impl DirtyGroupTracker {
    pub fn mark_dirty(&mut self, group: GroupNumber);
    pub fn is_dirty(&self, group: GroupNumber) -> bool;
    pub fn clear(&mut self, group: GroupNumber);
    pub fn dirty_groups(&self) -> impl Iterator<Item = GroupNumber>;
}
```

### 2. Refresh Triggers
- **Lazy**: Background scrub regenerates symbols for dirty groups
- **Eager (fsync)**: Regenerate immediately for committed groups
- **Configurable**: MountConfig.repair_refresh_policy

### 3. Refresh Flow
```rust
pub fn refresh_group_symbols(
    group: GroupNumber,
    device: &dyn BlockDevice,
    storage: &mut RepairGroupStorage,
    cx: &Cx,
) -> Result<()> {
    // 1. Read all blocks in group
    // 2. Regenerate RaptorQ symbols
    // 3. Write to repair storage slots
    // 4. Clear dirty flag
}
```

### 4. Budget Integration
- Refresh respects Cx budget
- Can be interrupted and resumed
- Progress tracked per-group

## TESTS
1. Unit: Dirty tracking set/clear
2. Integration: Write marks group dirty
3. Integration: Scrub refreshes dirty group
4. Integration: fsync refreshes if eager mode
5. E2E: Corruption after refresh -> recovery works

## LOGGING
- Mark dirty (trace): group number, cause
- Refresh start (debug): group, block count
- Refresh complete (info): group, duration, symbol count

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
1. Every modified group eventually refreshed
2. Lazy mode: refreshed within scrub interval
3. Eager mode: refreshed on fsync
4. Recovery works for refreshed groups","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T02:54:43.407560046Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-17T14:08:46.778165832Z","closed_at":"2026-02-17T14:08:46.778143631Z","close_reason":"Implemented dirty-group refresh APIs (mark/query/flush), aligned refresh logging, and added flush-path + tracker tests in ffs-repair pipeline; validated via rch cargo test/check/clippy and cargo fmt --check.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cow","raptorq","repair"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-820","depends_on_id":"bd-15c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:56:20.818428330Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-820","depends_on_id":"bd-22w.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:51:49.899554131Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":191,"issue_id":"bd-820","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Alien uplift contract (auto-applied via extreme-software-optimization + alien-artifact-coding + alien-graveyard).

 {"id":"bd-85ja","title":"Implement btrfs transparent decompression (ZLIB + ZSTD)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-22T18:00:35.647767488Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T18:00:49.704792242Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T18:00:49.704020800Z","close_reason":"Implemented btrfs transparent decompression in the read path:
- Added btrfs_decompress() dispatcher for ZLIB (type 1) and ZSTD (type 3) via flate2 and zstd crates
- LZO (type 2) returns clear UnsupportedFeature error (custom framing format, deferred)
- Updated both inline and regular extent read paths to decompress instead of rejecting
- Added ram_bytes field to BtrfsExtentData enum (Inline and Regular variants) for decompressed size tracking
- Updated parse_extent_data to read ram_bytes from offset 8 of extent item
- Updated to_bytes serialization to write ram_bytes correctly
- Fixed 10 BtrfsExtentData construction sites across ffs-btrfs and ffs-core
- Updated 2 tests from expect-unsupported to expect-corruption (invalid compression data detection)
471 ffs-core tests + 117 ffs-btrfs tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","compression","parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-89x","title":"Docs: Align toolchain/MSRV statements (Rust 2024, nightly toolchain)","description":"Goal: ensure docs do not state an impossible MSRV.

Context:
- Rust edition 2024 requires Rust >= 1.85.
- The repo uses rust-toolchain.toml for nightly.

Deliverables:
- Ensure PLAN/README/COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC consistently state the toolchain policy.
- If we claim an MSRV, it must be technically compatible with edition + dependencies.

Acceptance:
- No doc claims a Rust version that cannot compile the workspace.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:35:11.239312918Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T02:06:53.885173467Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T02:06:53.885096573Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-89x","depends_on_id":"bd-2ds","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:35:19.764294386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-zge.8","title":"ffs-core: Integrate ffs-journal replay into ext4 mount path + crash-recovery conformance","description":"Goal: make journal replay observable to users by wiring the new ffs-journal engine into the ext4 open/mount pipeline and proving behavior with deterministic crash-recovery tests.

Scope:
- Integrate replay_jbd2 invocation into ffs-core ext4 open flow behind compatibility-mode guard.
- Define journal-region discovery from ext4 superblock metadata (journal inode/device pointers) with explicit unsupported-mode errors.
- Surface replay metrics (ReplayStats) through engine diagnostics/logging path for auditable mount decisions.
- Add unit tests for integration glue (invocation conditions, error mapping, no-op behavior when journal absent).
- Add harness-level crash/recovery test flow that simulates committed and uncommitted journal transactions and verifies post-mount state.
- Add detailed structured logging lines for replay decisions and outcomes (scanned, committed, revoked, skipped).

Acceptance:
- Mount/open of ext4 fixture with journal transactions replays committed updates and ignores uncommitted/revoked updates.
- cargo test -p ffs-core and cargo test -p ffs-harness -- --nocapture include replay-path coverage with deterministic assertions.
- Logs contain machine-parseable replay summary fields suitable for CI diagnostics.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:21:35.744999433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:35:12.471070906Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:35:12.471036722Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","semantics"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-zge.8","depends_on_id":"bd-zge","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:21:35.744999433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-zge.8","depends_on_id":"bd-zge.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-11T06:21:35.744999433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
